page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8581C processor cores running at 2.3 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits strong single-core capabilities, achieving top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core execution. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show relative weaknesses. The Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 10 percent, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and stress-ng multi-core performance are positioned in the bottom 25 percent. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths average 38.47 GB/sec and 25.63 GB/sec, respectively.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, alongside cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI. These features assist in specialized tasks like low-latency text processing and small-scale model inference, where it processes 624.01 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model. Given the compute-heavy, low-memory profile, this instance is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic database operations, though it is constrained for memory-intensive applications or large-scale multi-threaded compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPU resources. The hardware profile excels in single-core tasks, delivering top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core benchmarks. Conversely, multi-threaded scaling and memory performance are weaker, with Passmark memory scores in the bottom 10 percent. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations, which aid in processing workloads like lightweight text processing and small-scale LLM inference. This instance is best suited for compute-bound, single-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations where high memory capacity is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and advanced AVX-512 and AMX support.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, strong single-core performance, and advanced matrix extension support for targeted workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors at 2.3 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations
Suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and small-scale inference
No local storage or bundled GPU resources included
